Project.NAME: japan-travel-rag-mcp
Project.keywords: japan-travel, mcp, fastmcp, oauth-proxy, logto, user-portal, landing, onboarding, root-auth, zod, contract-first, usage-tracking, drizzle, postgresql, site-sources, curated-sites, spider-cloud, voyage-4, tiktoken, worker-runtime, hybrid-search
Project.annotation: Portal-first architecture with seven MCP tools (4 proxied + 3 local), shareable onboarding UX, and worker-driven curated-sites crawl/index pipeline with attributed local retrieval.

M-CONFIG.NAME: Config
M-CONFIG.purpose: Runtime config validation for MCP OAuth proxy settings, admin auth, portal session/identity settings, M2M provisioning credentials, and database connection.

fn-loadConfig.description: Validate env and build typed AppConfig including DATABASE_URL.

type-AppConfig.description: App config for tg-chat-rag, admin token, portal session, public URL, Logto credentials, M2M provisioning credentials, databaseUrl, and unified proxy keys for Spider/Voyage.

M-LOGGER.NAME: Logger
M-LOGGER.purpose: Structured logger factory used by runtime, route handlers, and integration modules.

fn-createLogger.description: Create scoped logger with semantic block labels.

M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS.NAME: ToolContracts
M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS.purpose: Tool schema registry and validators for proxied tg-chat tools and local curated-site tools.

schema-SearchMessagesInputPublic.description: Public schema for search_messages with filters.chat_ids forbidden.

const-TOOL_INPUT_JSON_SCHEMAS.description: Tool descriptions and parameter contracts consumed by runtime registration.

M-SITE-SOURCES.NAME: SiteSources
M-SITE-SOURCES.purpose: Serve curated source registry from `site_sources` database table with bootstrap seed constant used only for initialization.

const-SITE_SOURCES_RESPONSE.description: Bootstrap seed data: description_and_tiers + initial source entries.

fn-getSiteSources.description: Fetch registry rows from `site_sources` table for local tool response.

fn-upsertSiteSourcesSeed.description: Seed/refresh initial source rows from bootstrap constant.

schema-GetSiteSourcesInputSchema.description: Empty z.object({}) schema for FastMCP tool registration.

type-SiteSource.description: Source entry: source_id, name, domain, tier, language, focus, status, crawl_interval_minutes, max_pages.

M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT.NAME: SpiderCloudClient
M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT.purpose: Spider crawl API adapter routed through unified proxy for scheduled and targeted recrawl jobs.

fn-runCrawl.description: Execute crawl request via `${PROXY_BASE_URL}/api.spider.cloud/*` with proxy and provider auth headers.

M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT.NAME: VoyageProxyClient
M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT.purpose: Voyage embedding adapter routed through unified proxy for query/document embedding workflows.

fn-embedDocuments.description: Generate `voyage-4` document embeddings via proxy endpoint.

fn-embedQuery.description: Generate `voyage-4` query embedding via proxy endpoint.

M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY.NAME: SitesIndexRepository
M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY.purpose: Persist curated-site pages/chunks/embeddings and serve hybrid search/chunk lookup queries with active index version filtering.

fn-upsertPage.description: Upsert normalized page records by canonical URL/hash.

fn-upsertChunks.description: Upsert deterministic chunk records with chunking/index versions.

fn-upsertEmbeddings.description: Upsert chunk embeddings for active index version.

fn-searchHybrid.description: Run vector + FTS search with active index_version filter.

fn-getChunkWithNeighbors.description: Resolve bounded chunk excerpt and optional neighbors.

M-SITES-PARSER.NAME: SitesParser
M-SITES-PARSER.purpose: Parse Spider crawl payload into canonical URL/title/clean-text records.

fn-parseCrawlItem.description: Normalize crawl item to deterministic page representation.

M-SITES-CHUNKER.NAME: SitesChunker
M-SITES-CHUNKER.purpose: Deterministic structural + token split chunking for curated-site text.

fn-chunkPage.description: Apply v1 chunking config (`target=450`, `max=650`, `overlap=80`) with npm `tiktoken` token counting.

M-SITES-INGESTION.NAME: SitesIngestionOrchestrator
M-SITES-INGESTION.purpose: Coordinate crawl-only ingestion, parsing, chunking, embedding, and repository upserts for curated sites.
M-SITES-INGESTION.depends: M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT, M-SITES-PARSER, M-SITES-CHUNKER, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-LOGGER

fn-runScheduledIngestion.description: Run due-source ingestion based on source intervals.

fn-runTargetedRecrawl.description: Run internal single-URL recrawl through crawl endpoint with strict cap.

M-SITES-SEARCH.NAME: SitesSearchService
M-SITES-SEARCH.purpose: Serve local `search_sites` and `get_page_chunk` tool logic over local indexed content.

fn-searchSites.description: Run hybrid retrieval and return attributed ranked snippets.

fn-getPageChunk.description: Return bounded chunk excerpt with optional neighbors.

M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.NAME: TgChatRagClient
M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.purpose: HTTP methods adapter for tg-chat-rag upstream API.

type-UpstreamCallError.description: Normalized upstream error surface for protocol/timeout/http failures.

M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH.NAME: ConsentPatch
M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH.purpose: Replace FastMCP built-in consent screen with portal-styled HTML via monkey-patching OAuthProxy.consentManager.

fn-generatePortalConsentScreen.description: Render portal-styled consent HTML with scope labels, XSS escaping, and form POST to /oauth/consent.

fn-formatScopeLabel.description: Map OAuth scope strings to human-readable labels.

M-AUTH-PROXY.NAME: FastMcpOauthProxy
M-AUTH-PROXY.purpose: FastMCP OAuthProxy integration for /mcp authentication backed by Logto OIDC upstream endpoints.

fn-createOauthProxy.description: Create OAuthProxy, apply portal consent patch, and provide authorization-server metadata for FastMCP oauth config.

type-OauthProxyDeps.description: Validated dependency bundle for proxy construction.

M-PORTAL-IDENTITY.NAME: PortalIdentityClient
M-PORTAL-IDENTITY.purpose: Identity-provider adapter for portal social OAuth start/callback flows and automatic tester access role assignment.

fn-buildPortalOauthStartUrl.description: Build provider-specific Logto authorization URL for social direct sign-in.

fn-handlePortalOauthCallback.description: Exchange callback code and resolve tester identity profile.

fn-ensureMcpAccessRole.description: Assign/verify MCP user role via dedicated M2M app credentials and configurable role ID without manual admin actions.

M-TOOL-PROXY.NAME: ToolProxyService
M-TOOL-PROXY.purpose: Tool execution orchestration: validate input, apply policy, call upstream, normalize MCP result.

fn-executeTool.description: Main proxy execution method for all supported tools.

fn-buildUpstreamPayloadWithPolicy.description: Inject internal chat scope for search_messages.

fn-authenticateAdmin.description: Validate login token and issue signed session cookie.

fn-requireAdminSession.description: Allow/deny decision for protected admin routes.

fn-clearAdminSession.description: Generate cookie invalidation header for logout.

M-PORTAL-AUTH.NAME: PortalAuth
M-PORTAL-AUTH.purpose: Portal tester session lifecycle management and route guards for /portal protected routes.

fn-authenticatePortalUser.description: Issue portal session after successful identity auth.

fn-requirePortalSession.description: Allow or redirect for protected portal routes.

fn-clearPortalSession.description: Invalidate tester portal session cookie on logout.

M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING.NAME: PortalProvisioning
M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING.purpose: Automatic post-registration/login provisioning for tester MCP access rights.

fn-provisionTesterAccess.description: Ensure required MCP tester role/policy exists for user.

M-USAGE-TRACKER.NAME: UsageTracker
M-USAGE-TRACKER.purpose: Track per-user MCP tool call counts in PostgreSQL via Drizzle UPSERT and expose query interface for portal display.

fn-createUsageTracker.description: Build usage tracker backed by Drizzle PostgreSQL with auto-schema bootstrap.

fn-recordToolCall.description: Increment per-user per-tool counter via UPSERT; fire-and-forget with warning on failure.

fn-getUserStats.description: Query all per-tool counters and total for a given user ID.

type-UsageTracker.description: Usage tracking interface for tool call recording and stats queries.

type-UserUsageStats.description: Per-user usage statistics payload for portal rendering.

schema-usageCountersTable.description: Drizzle pg-core schema: usage_counters(user_id, tool_name, call_count, last_called_at) with composite PK.

M-ADMIN-UI.NAME: AdminUiRoutes
M-ADMIN-UI.purpose: Server-rendered admin login and ops routes guarded by AdminAuth.

M-PORTAL-UI.NAME: PortalUiRoutes
M-PORTAL-UI.purpose: Server-rendered landing + portal per-page routes for external tester onboarding with progressive enhancement and per-user usage statistics display.
M-PORTAL-UI.depends: M-PORTAL-AUTH, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING, M-PORTAL-IDENTITY, M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-USAGE-TRACKER

fn-handleLandingRequest.description: Serve / landing with redirect action to portal.

fn-handlePortalRootRoute.description: Handle /portal and route to login/home by portal session state.

fn-handlePortalRegisterRoute.description: Handle /portal/register social-provider page without password fields.

fn-handlePortalLoginRoute.description: Handle /portal/login social-provider page without password fields.

fn-handlePortalOauthStartRoute.description: Handle /portal/auth/start and redirect to Logto social OAuth endpoint.

fn-handlePortalOauthCallbackRoute.description: Handle /portal/auth/callback and create portal session after provisioning.

fn-handlePortalHomeRoute.description: Handle /portal/home authenticated onboarding view with per-user usage statistics.

fn-renderPortalLayout.description: Render shared portal page shell and include compiled Tailwind stylesheet asset.

fn-renderPortalConnectionGuide.description: Render MCP setup instructions for external AI agents.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.NAME: FastMcpRuntime
M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.purpose: Construct FastMCP runtime, register fixed tool surface (4 proxied + 3 local) with per-user usage tracking, configure OAuth Proxy on /mcp, and mount admin + portal routes.
M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.depends: M-AUTH-PROXY, M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-UI, M-USAGE-TRACKER, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SITES-SEARCH

fn-createFastMcpRuntime.description: Build FastMCP instance with oauth proxy and route extension.

fn-registerProxyTools.description: Register four proxied tool handlers with Zod schemas and per-user usage tracking via context.session JWT sub extraction.

fn-registerLocalTools.description: Register local tools (`get_site_sources`, `search_sites`, `get_page_chunk`) with canAccess guard, local execute handlers, and usage tracking.

fn-extractUserIdFromSession.description: Decode JWT sub claim from RuntimeOauthSession idToken or accessToken for usage tracking.

fn-mountAdminRoutes.description: Mount /admin routes through Hono app returned by getApp().

fn-mountPortalRoutes.description: Mount /, /portal, and /portal/* routes through Hono app returned by getApp().

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain
M-SERVER.purpose: Bootstrap API runtime config/dependencies, then start FastMCP HTTP stream runtime with MCP, admin, portal surfaces, usage tracking, and local tools.
M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-IDENTITY, M-PORTAL-AUTH, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING, M-PORTAL-UI, M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME, M-DB, M-USAGE-TRACKER, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SITES-SEARCH, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT

fn-main.description: Initialize API runtime dependencies, mount route handlers, and start server lifecycle.

M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT.NAME: WorkerMain
M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT.purpose: Bootstrap worker runtime and execute recurring curated-site scheduling/ingestion/index jobs.
M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-DB, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT, M-SITES-PARSER, M-SITES-CHUNKER, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-SITES-INGESTION

fn-main.description: Run scheduler loop for crawl-only ingestion and index refresh jobs.

M-ROOT-ENTRYPOINT.NAME: RootEntrypoint
M-ROOT-ENTRYPOINT.purpose: Top-level process bootstrap and fatal error mapping.

fn-bootstrap.description: Invoke main() and terminate process on fatal startup error.

M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.NAME: McpAuthProvider
M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.purpose: Legacy mcp-auth provider integration scheduled for removal after OAuth Proxy migration.

fn-initMcpAuth.description: Legacy provider init kept only during transition.

M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.NAME: McpAuthAdapter
M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.purpose: Legacy authenticate(request) bridge scheduled for removal after OAuth Proxy migration.

fn-authenticateFastMcpRequest.description: Legacy request auth adapter for mcp-auth flow.

M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.NAME: ApiKeyRepository
M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.purpose: Legacy API-key repository outside simplified runtime target.

M-DB.NAME: DrizzleDb
M-DB.purpose: Drizzle PostgreSQL client bootstrap with fail-fast connectivity probe for usage tracking and future persistence modules.

fn-createDb.description: Validate DATABASE_URL, initialize pg Pool + Drizzle, verify connectivity, and return DbClient.

type-DbClient.description: Typed Drizzle database and pg Pool handles for repository modules.

type-DbConnectionError.description: Typed database bootstrap error with DB_CONNECTION_ERROR code.

DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.NAME: portal onboarding flow
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-1: User opens `/portal/register` or `/portal/login` and chooses social provider.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-2: `/portal/auth/start` redirects to Logto social OAuth flow and returns to `/portal/auth/callback`.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-3: M-PORTAL-IDENTITY resolves identity, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING ensures MCP role, and M-PORTAL-AUTH issues session.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-4: M-PORTAL-UI renders MCP setup guide after successful callback flow.

DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.NAME: portal progressive enhancement flow
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-1: Portal page is rendered on server using Bun JSX templates.
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-2: HTMX updates social-provider selection/error fragments and can trigger OAuth start route requests.
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-3: Alpine.js handles optional local UI state only (for example toggles/copy helpers).

DF-MCP-OAUTH.NAME: mcp oauth proxy flow
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-1: Client starts OAuth flow through FastMCP endpoints on `/mcp`.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-2: M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH renders portal-styled consent screen for user approval.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-3: After consent, M-AUTH-PROXY redirects to Logto upstream for authentication.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-4: M-AUTH-PROXY exchanges code/tokens with Logto and issues proxy session.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-5: Authenticated session can execute registered MCP tools.

DF-TOOL-PROXY.NAME: tool proxy flow
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-3: M-TOOL-PROXY applies policy and calls M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-4: Normalized result is returned via MCP transport.

DF-USAGE-TRACKING.NAME: per-user tool call usage tracking flow
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-1: MCP tool execute handler receives context.session with RuntimeOauthSession.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-2: extractUserIdFromSession decodes idToken/accessToken JWT and extracts sub claim.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-3: M-USAGE-TRACKER.recordToolCall(userId, toolName) performs UPSERT on usage_counters.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-4: Tracking failures are caught and logged; tool result is always returned.

DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.NAME: portal usage statistics display flow
DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.step-3: Portal home renders per-tool call counts and total in usage section.

DF-SITE-SOURCES.NAME: get_site_sources local tool flow
DF-SITE-SOURCES.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls get_site_sources with no parameters.
DF-SITE-SOURCES.step-3: Local execute handler returns registry rows from `site_sources` table (bootstrap constant is seed-only).

DF-SITES-INGESTION.NAME: curated sites crawl ingestion flow
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-1: M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT scheduler selects due sources from M-SITE-SOURCES.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-2: M-SITES-INGESTION calls M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT crawl endpoint through unified proxy.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-3: M-SITES-PARSER and M-SITES-CHUNKER normalize and split fetched content.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-4: M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT creates embeddings and M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY persists index artifacts.

DF-SITES-SEARCH.NAME: search_sites local tool flow
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls `search_sites` via M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME local tool route.
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-2: M-SITES-SEARCH builds query embedding via M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT.
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-3: M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY executes hybrid vector+FTS search with active index version filter.
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-4: Attributed snippet results are returned with source_id, tier, URL, and chunk_id.

DF-PAGE-CHUNK.NAME: get_page_chunk local tool flow
DF-PAGE-CHUNK.step-2: M-SITES-SEARCH resolves chunk + neighbors through M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY.
DF-PAGE-CHUNK.step-3: Response returns bounded excerpt only (no full-page dump).

DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.NAME: admin root auth flow
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH verifies `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` and issues session cookie.
